title: Junior Cloud Engineer
description: Support the cloud platform team by provisioning and maintaining AWS environments for application squads. Write and review small Terraform modules, debug container deployment failures, and shadow senior engineers during on-call to learn incident workflows.

title: Junior Cloud Engineer
description: Work alongside senior engineers on a phased GCP migration, moving workloads out of legacy VMs into managed services. Automate repetitive provisioning and audit tasks with shell and Python scripts, and help document runbooks for the wider engineering org.

title: Junior Cloud Engineer
description: Provision and tear down Azure environments for internal product teams using ARM templates and Terraform. Learn container orchestration fundamentals by running sandbox workloads and participating in platform design reviews led by senior engineers.

title: Associate Cloud Engineer
description: Operate AWS EC2 compute fleets and storage buckets for a mid-sized product org, handling capacity requests and storage lifecycle policies. Contribute to shared CI/CD pipelines and join a rotating on-call schedule for non-production incidents.

title: Cloud Engineer
description: Design and operate multi-region AWS footprints that serve customer traffic across North America and Europe. Manage Kubernetes workloads and Helm charts for internal platform services, and partner with application teams on capacity, failover, and release strategy.

title: Cloud Engineer
description: Own the GCP infrastructure-as-code codebase end-to-end for a platform serving dozens of product teams. Implement and tune observability with Prometheus and Grafana, define SLOs with service owners, and lead primary on-call for platform incidents.

title: Cloud Engineer
description: Design event-driven pipelines on AWS Lambda to decouple asynchronous workflows for the core product. Harden IAM policies and S3 bucket configurations, lead quarterly cost reviews, and drive down idle-resource spend across the org.

title: Cloud Engineer II
description: Operate production AKS clusters for a regulated customer-facing product, tuning upgrade strategy and node-pool layout for reliability. Author reusable Terraform modules for the platform team's catalog and run technical reviews for consuming application teams.

title: Cloud Engineer II
description: Maintain a hybrid AWS and GCP footprint as the company gradually standardizes on Kubernetes across both providers. Drive migrations off legacy EC2 instances, run architecture reviews with product teams, and own runbooks for cross-cloud failure modes.

title: Senior Cloud Engineer
description: Lead cloud architecture decisions across a multi-account AWS organization, setting patterns for landing zones, networking, and service segmentation. Mentor mid-level engineers on Terraform and Kubernetes best practices and act as the tie-breaker on cross-team design debates.

title: Senior Cloud Engineer
description: Drive GCP platform strategy for a fast-growing org, setting direction on project hierarchy, networking, and shared services. Own the IAM and compliance posture across production projects, and lead org-wide initiatives on cost efficiency and reliability maturity.

title: Staff Cloud Engineer
description: Architect the AWS platform that supports a 200-engineer organization across multiple product lines. Sponsor cross-team reliability programs, partner with leadership on vendor and commitment strategy, and represent the platform team in architecture governance forums.

title: Principal Cloud Engineer
description: Set multi-cloud engineering standards across AWS and GCP and drive a unified platform roadmap reviewed quarterly with leadership. Own org-wide infrastructure-as-code conventions, mentor senior engineers on platform strategy, and advise product leadership on cloud cost and risk.

title: Junior Security Analyst
description: Triage incoming alerts in the SIEM, classify severity, and escalate confirmed incidents per established runbooks. Contribute to detection tuning under senior guidance, and help maintain and expand the team's incident response runbook library for new attack patterns.

title: Junior SOC Analyst
description: Monitor Splunk dashboards on a 24/7 follow-the-sun rotation, identifying anomalous activity and escalating per established playbooks. Assist senior analysts during tabletop exercises, author post-shift summaries, and contribute to weekly detection review meetings.

title: Associate Security Analyst
description: Support the vulnerability management program by running scans, triaging findings, and coordinating remediation with engineering owners. Track remediation SLAs across teams and work on internal pentesting labs to build hands-on offensive skills under senior guidance.

title: Junior Application Security Analyst
description: Review code changes and service designs for OWASP top-10 risks as part of an application security rotation with engineering teams. Run SAST tooling, file detailed bug reports with reproduction steps and actionable remediation guidance, and shadow senior engineers during threat reviews.

title: Security Analyst
description: Own SIEM content engineering for the security operations team, writing and maintaining detection rules backed by tests. Lead incident investigations end-to-end from alert through root cause, and partner with platform teams to harden IAM policies based on observed attack surface.

title: Security Analyst II
description: Run the SOC's monthly purple-team cycle, coordinating simulated attack chains with the red team and detection coverage work with the blue team. Lead external pentest engagements, triage findings, and drive remediation across engineering owners to a clear SLA.

title: Security Analyst II
description: Operate the vulnerability management program at scale across thousands of assets, including scanning, prioritization, and remediation coordination. Drive SOC 2 readiness work alongside the GRC team, maintaining evidence and audit artifacts across quarterly control reviews.

title: Security Analyst II
description: Lead threat modeling reviews for new services during architecture design, capturing trust boundaries and deriving concrete security requirements. Own the application security backlog with engineering partners, prioritizing findings by blast radius and driving remediation across teams.

title: Security Analyst III
description: Develop and maintain detection-as-code pipelines so Splunk rule changes flow through code review, tests, and CI before hitting production. Own the full Splunk content lifecycle and build IR automation to shave minutes off high-volume playbook steps.

title: Senior Security Analyst
description: Lead the detection and response program for a growing security organization, setting priorities across intake, triage, and investigation. Mentor junior analysts, set and evolve SOC playbooks, and act as incident commander for high-severity investigations.

title: Senior Security Engineer
description: Drive application security strategy across multiple product lines, balancing threat modeling coverage, pentest cadence, and compliance evidence work. Advise executives on risk posture and partner with engineering leaders on security requirements baked into product planning.

title: Lead Security Analyst
description: Own the SOC's leadership rotation and act as a senior incident commander for major events. Drive incident response maturity through postmortem programs, tabletop cadence, and a purple-team program that spans detection, offense, and platform engineering.

title: Principal Security Analyst
description: Set and maintain org-wide security standards spanning application security, incident response, and governance, risk, and compliance. Partner with executives on quarterly risk reporting, board-level security narratives, and strategic investments across the security function.

title: Junior Backend Engineer
description: Build REST endpoints in a Python and Flask service, implementing request validation, error handling, and unit tests for new routes. Learn Postgres schema design and query optimization under senior guidance, and participate in weekly code reviews and pair-programming sessions.

title: Junior Backend Engineer
description: Ship user-facing features inside a long-lived Django monolith, including ORM model changes, templated views, and background job handlers. Pair with senior engineers on code reviews, ramp into an on-call rotation, and contribute to incremental service extraction work as it comes up.

title: Associate Backend Engineer
description: Develop Node.js services on Express for a product backend, focusing on request validation, error handling, and integration tests. Contribute to MongoDB data model refinements, propose indexing changes under senior review, and pair with the data platform team on read patterns.

title: Associate Backend Engineer
description: Implement features in a Go service supporting a high-traffic product area, with emphasis on clean interfaces and comprehensive test coverage. Contribute to internal tooling that the rest of the backend team uses daily, and participate in code review rotations with senior engineers.

title: Backend Engineer
description: Own a Python and FastAPI service end-to-end, shaping its API surface, operational posture, and test coverage as requirements evolve. Design Postgres schemas for new product features, optimize slow queries, and partner with SREs on reliability and capacity planning.

title: Backend Engineer
description: Build and operate high-throughput Go microservices that sit on the critical path for product traffic. Own service reliability and observability for the team's services, including SLO definition, tracing instrumentation, and postmortem follow-ups after incidents.

title: Backend Engineer II
description: Develop Java and Spring Boot services within a larger platform composed of dozens of interacting services. Drive API design reviews with consumers, author and maintain contract tests across service boundaries, and lead incremental refactors that improve modularity.

title: Backend Engineer II
description: Own significant portions of a Ruby on Rails application backend, shipping features across payments, billing, and user management. Collaborate with product managers on requirements, own schema migrations in a high-write database, and mentor incoming engineers on Rails conventions.

title: Backend Engineer II
description: Ship Python services with FastAPI supporting a new product area, including auth, persistence, and external integrations. Lead the team's first GraphQL adoption, balancing schema design, caching, and client ergonomics with existing REST consumers.

title: Senior Backend Engineer
description: Lead service design reviews across a backend team responsible for several product-critical services. Mentor mid-level engineers on API ergonomics, Postgres patterns, and testing strategy, and act as the tie-breaker on cross-team contract changes.

title: Senior Backend Engineer
description: Own the technical output of a Go-focused platform team powering internal infrastructure used by application engineers. Drive cross-team contract design, reliability reviews, and capacity planning for a service estate with aggressive uptime targets.

title: Staff Backend Engineer
description: Architect data-plane services in Java for a large, high-throughput platform where latency and correctness both matter. Sponsor multi-team initiatives around schema evolution, observability, and capacity, and represent the team in cross-org architecture reviews.

title: Principal Backend Engineer
description: Set backend engineering standards across the organization, covering language choices, API design, and service lifecycle. Own API governance and reliability programs, partner with leadership on multi-quarter platform roadmaps, and mentor staff-level engineers across teams.

title: Junior Data Analyst
description: Write SQL to answer ad-hoc questions from stakeholders across the growth org, translating fuzzy business questions into precise queries. Build Tableau dashboards that surface funnel, retention, and acquisition metrics, and iterate on them with PMs as their needs evolve.

title: Junior Data Analyst
description: Support the finance team with recurring SQL reporting on revenue, cost, and forecasting inputs, and explain methodology to non-technical stakeholders. Learn experimentation fundamentals and statistical reasoning alongside senior analytics leads, and shadow them during readouts.

title: Associate Analyst
description: Build and maintain marketing funnel dashboards in Looker, covering acquisition, activation, and retention across paid and organic channels. Partner with growth PMs on KPI definitions, diagnose dashboard anomalies, and document analytical choices for the rest of the team.

title: Junior Business Analyst
description: Own weekly operations reporting delivered through Power BI dashboards consumed by operations leadership. Collaborate with operations leads on data quality issues upstream, investigate discrepancies with engineering partners, and present findings at the weekly business review.

title: Data Analyst
description: Lead analytics for a product team, partnering with PMs on roadmap decisions grounded in user behavior and experiment evidence. Design experiments end-to-end, compute and communicate results to stakeholders, and push back when interpretations outrun what the data can support.

title: Data Analyst
description: Drive self-serve analytics adoption across product and marketing teams by curating trusted explores and documenting metric definitions. Own Looker models, review incoming LookML contributions from other analysts, and host office hours for consumers of the analytics stack.

title: Data Analyst II
description: Partner with finance leadership on forecasting models, working through methodology, inputs, and confidence intervals. Build automated reporting via Python and dbt so finance leadership can drill into variance against forecast without waiting on manual refreshes.

title: Data Analyst II
description: Own experimentation platform adoption across product teams, helping them design well-powered tests and avoid common pitfalls. Perform statistical analysis and lift estimation for high-visibility launches and push methodology improvements upstream to the platform team.

title: Data Analyst II
description: Drive analytics for marketing attribution, combining on-platform events with partner data to tell a trustworthy story about channel performance. Build models in dbt on Snowflake, document assumptions clearly, and partner with marketing leadership on campaign postmortems.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: Lead product analytics for a growing engineering organization, framing questions with PMs and picking the right methodology for each. Mentor junior analysts on experimentation rigor and causal inference, and represent analytics in cross-functional launch planning.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: Own analytics strategy for the finance organization, aligning reporting, forecasting, and investigative analytics with leadership priorities. Partner directly with executives on board-ready narratives and coach finance partners on how to get the most from the analytics team.

title: Lead Analyst
description: Run the cross-org analytics guild, setting tooling standards and convening recurring forums on methodology and tradecraft. Drive data literacy training for partner teams and advise leadership on analytics hiring, career frameworks, and investment priorities.

title: Principal Data Analyst
description: Advise executives on business performance and strategy through a combination of recurring reviews and targeted deep-dives. Own executive dashboards and board-level narrative work, and shape the organization's point of view on its most important metrics.

title: Junior ML Engineer
description: Ship modeling features and pipeline updates with scikit-learn under senior guidance on a product team using classical ML. Learn experiment tracking with MLflow, contribute to evaluation notebooks, and participate in regular model review sessions alongside more experienced ML engineers.

title: Junior ML Engineer
description: Build baseline models in PyTorch for a research-adjacent product team tackling new modeling problems. Assist with data pipelines and evaluation notebooks under senior guidance, and participate in weekly paper readings to build foundational context.

title: Associate ML Engineer
description: Support NLP feature development for a search and ranking team under senior guidance and review. Run fine-tuning experiments on Hugging Face models, maintain evaluation datasets, and contribute to offline eval notebooks used in model launch decisions.

title: Associate ML Engineer
description: Train computer vision models in TensorFlow as part of a team building defect detection for an industrial product. Contribute to training data pipelines, including labeling workflows and data augmentation, and assist with evaluation on held-out benchmark sets.

title: ML Engineer
description: Own tabular ML models end-to-end, from feature engineering and training through evaluation, deployment, and monitoring. Track experiments with MLflow, work with data engineers on reliable feature pipelines, and defend model choices in launch reviews.

title: ML Engineer
description: Train and deploy deep learning models across the PyTorch ecosystem, handling mixed GPU and CPU infrastructure for training and serving. Partner with platform teams on efficient utilization and own offline-to-online evaluation discipline for model launches.

title: ML Engineer II
description: Own NLP systems for search and recommendation surfaces, combining Hugging Face models with custom transformer work tailored to product requirements. Drive eval methodology, ship regular model updates, and partner with product on how the model's behavior maps to user outcomes.

title: ML Engineer II
description: Productionize computer vision models for an industrial quality inspection system deployed at multiple factory sites. Build data pipelines with Spark, own the offline evaluation pipeline, and partner with onsite ops teams on labeling, data quality, and deployment rollouts.

title: ML Engineer II
description: Build experimentation platform components for a growing org, including randomization, stats, and reporting layers. Support A/B testing, causal inference, and ML model evaluation use cases, and partner with product analysts to keep methodology consistent across teams.

title: Senior ML Engineer
description: Lead ML systems for a product area, setting direction on modeling approach, eval practice, and production integration. Mentor junior ML engineers on production patterns, partner with product managers on launch readiness, and run cross-team model reviews.

title: Senior ML Engineer
description: Own the company's NLP platform, covering training, serving, and eval surfaces used by multiple product teams. Partner with product leaders on launch decisions grounded in rigorous offline and online evaluation, and advise on modeling tradeoffs at planning time.

title: Staff ML Engineer
description: Architect ML infrastructure used by multiple product teams, spanning training, serving, and artifact management. Drive modeling standards and evaluation practices across teams, mentor staff-track ICs, and partner with platform leadership on multi-quarter roadmaps.

title: Principal ML Engineer
description: Set ML strategy across product lines, weighing modeling ambition against shipping constraints and platform maturity. Partner with research leadership and product executives on portfolio decisions, and mentor senior ML engineers across the organization.

title: Junior Frontend Engineer
description: Build React UI components inside an established design system used by multiple product teams. Learn accessibility fundamentals through code review, contribute to the internal component library, and participate in cross-team design reviews with senior frontend engineers.

title: Junior Frontend Engineer
description: Ship user-facing features inside a Next.js application that powers the main marketing and commerce funnels. Pair with senior frontend engineers during code review, participate in design critique sessions, and learn Next.js rendering tradeoffs through hands-on work.

title: Associate Frontend Engineer
description: Implement UI features in a Vue 3 single-page application powering an internal workflow tool. Contribute to the shared component library alongside senior engineers, and participate in design reviews to learn how product requirements translate into component boundaries.

title: Associate Frontend Engineer
description: Build Angular components for an enterprise dashboard product used by large customers with complex configuration needs. Work with senior engineers on state management patterns and enterprise customer requirements, and learn the team's approach to regression testing and release coordination.

title: Frontend Engineer
description: Own React and Next.js features for growth surfaces where performance and accessibility directly affect acquisition. Drive Core Web Vitals and accessibility improvements via measurement and targeted interventions, and partner with the design team on component-level patterns.

title: Frontend Engineer
description: Ship features in a TypeScript-heavy React app where type safety and maintainability are taken seriously. Work closely with the design system team on new components, lead code reviews for mid-level peers, and own incremental improvements to testing and CI workflows.

title: Frontend Engineer II
description: Build a cross-browser single-page application with Vue 3 powering a product used across consumer and enterprise channels. Own build tooling, pipeline ergonomics, and frontend reliability, and partner with platform teams on observability for client-side errors.

title: Frontend Engineer II
description: Lead an Angular-to-React migration for a legacy product with years of accumulated UI, balancing feature velocity against platform transformation. Design an incremental adoption strategy that lets product teams ship during the migration and coordinate cross-team rollout.

title: Frontend Engineer II
description: Own the internal design system and ship accessible React primitives consumed by every product team in the org. Partner with designers on ergonomics and accessibility patterns, run adoption reviews with consumer teams, and shape the design system roadmap.

title: Senior Frontend Engineer
description: Lead frontend architecture for a product area, setting direction on rendering strategy, state management, and cross-team contracts. Mentor mid-level engineers on React patterns and TypeScript ergonomics, and represent the team in architecture reviews.

title: Senior Frontend Engineer
description: Own frontend performance and observability across a multi-team product, setting budgets and regression thresholds. Drive Core Web Vitals work org-wide through guided rollouts, tooling investments, and targeted coaching for teams owning slow surfaces.

title: Staff Frontend Engineer
description: Set frontend engineering standards across the organization, from framework choice to testing and release practices. Sponsor design-system adoption and type-safety initiatives that span multiple teams, and partner with engineering leadership on multi-quarter roadmaps.

title: Principal Frontend Engineer
description: Own frontend platform strategy across multiple product lines with distinct constraints and customer profiles. Partner with staff peers in backend and design systems, advise leadership on platform investment, and grow senior ICs through coaching and technical-writing feedback.

title: Junior DevOps Engineer
description: Maintain Jenkins pipelines for product teams, triaging pipeline failures and pairing with engineers on reliability fixes. Write Ansible playbooks for server configuration, handle routine operational tickets, and shadow senior DevOps engineers during on-call.

title: Junior DevOps Engineer
description: Support a company-wide CI/CD modernization effort moving pipelines onto GitHub Actions. Author new workflows under senior guidance, help migrate existing pipelines off legacy systems, and contribute to the team's documentation as the new patterns stabilize.

title: Associate DevOps Engineer
description: Contribute Terraform modules to the platform team's catalog, starting with low-risk refactors and expanding scope with experience. Run Kubernetes workloads in the platform environment, triage cluster-level issues with senior engineers, and participate in on-call shadowing.

title: Associate SRE
description: Support the observability platform on a junior SRE track, tuning Prometheus alerts to reduce noise and improve actionability. Work with application teams on Grafana dashboards for new services, and learn incident response patterns through shadowing senior SREs.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Own the CI/CD platform used across product engineering, setting conventions and operating the shared infrastructure. Lead the multi-quarter migration of legacy Jenkins pipelines to GitHub Actions, including tooling, documentation, and enablement work.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Run the Kubernetes platform used by application teams, including upgrade cadence, capacity, and shared tenancy concerns. Own Helm chart standards and review templates used org-wide, and partner with application engineers on deployment ergonomics.

title: DevOps Engineer II
description: Own the observability stack built on Prometheus and Grafana and used across all production services. Drive SLO adoption across teams through hands-on collaboration, recurring reviews, and tooling improvements that make healthy patterns the default choice.

title: DevOps Engineer II
description: Maintain the Ansible-based configuration management stack powering the company's legacy infrastructure footprint. Drive infrastructure-as-code maturity by migrating targeted areas to Terraform and growing the org's testing and review discipline for infra changes.

title: DevOps Engineer II
description: Run the Datadog-centric observability setup the company uses across product and platform teams. Partner with application teams on reliability improvements, SLO definition, and postmortem follow-through, and tune cost and ingestion for fast-growing services.

title: Senior DevOps Engineer
description: Lead the platform team's CI/CD roadmap across multiple product orgs, prioritizing investments across ergonomics, security, and reliability. Mentor junior engineers, drive Kubernetes adoption for latecomer teams, and own the multi-quarter migration plan.

title: Senior DevOps Engineer
description: Own the SRE practice for the company, setting expectations for error budgets, reliability reviews, and incident response behavior. Drive incident response culture through postmortem coaching, tabletop exercises, and cross-team reliability initiatives with real follow-through.

title: Staff DevOps Engineer
description: Set platform engineering standards across the company, covering tooling, patterns, and the lifecycle of shared infrastructure. Own multi-cloud infrastructure strategy in partnership with security and finance, and sponsor reliability programs across product engineering.

title: Principal DevOps Engineer
description: Architect the platform that a 300-engineer organization builds on, balancing developer experience, reliability, and cost. Sponsor multi-team reliability and developer-experience programs, and partner with leadership on vendor, staffing, and roadmap decisions.

title: Junior Data Engineer
description: Assist with Airflow DAG authoring for batch pipelines feeding analytics and ML surfaces. Learn dbt under senior guidance through hands-on model contributions, participate in code review rotations, and own pipeline monitoring duties on a rotating basis.

title: Junior Data Engineer
description: Build ingestion jobs in Python that land data from upstream systems into the warehouse at the required cadence. Contribute to Snowflake modeling work under senior guidance, write targeted tests against pipeline outputs, and help maintain operational runbooks.

title: Associate Data Engineer
description: Maintain BigQuery pipelines used by analytics and product teams, triaging upstream changes and schema drifts. Contribute to data quality tooling the team shares across pipelines, and pair with senior data engineers on more complex migrations and backfills.

title: Associate Data Engineer
description: Write Spark jobs powering data lake ETL for a high-volume consumer product. Learn Kafka and streaming concepts under senior guidance, run backfills with care, and contribute to the team's testing patterns for large-scale batch jobs.

title: Data Engineer
description: Own end-to-end Airflow and dbt pipelines on Snowflake that feed product analytics, finance, and ML workflows. Shape data models in partnership with downstream consumers, run incident response for pipeline failures, and drive data quality improvements upstream.

title: Data Engineer
description: Build streaming pipelines with Kafka and Spark for near-real-time product analytics and ML features. Own data contracts with upstream product teams, set expectations around schema evolution, and partner with SRE on reliability and backpressure patterns.

title: Data Engineer II
description: Own the BigQuery warehouse used by analytics and product teams across the company. Lead dbt adoption and testing culture, including model conventions, exposure tracking, and CI patterns that let data analysts contribute safely to the shared codebase.

title: Data Engineer II
description: Lead the migration of legacy ETL to Airflow plus dbt on Snowflake, running the effort in phased waves with product and analytics teams. Own lineage and data quality tooling across the new stack, and drive the team's investment in observability for data pipelines.

title: Data Engineer II
description: Run a Redshift plus dbt warehouse serving analytics and ML customers across the organization. Build streaming ingestion with Kafka for product events, and partner with analytics leadership on schema evolution and warehouse performance tuning.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description: Lead the data platform serving the analytics organization, setting priorities across pipelines, warehouse, and tooling. Mentor junior and mid-level data engineers on dbt and modeling patterns, and partner with analytics leadership on multi-quarter roadmaps.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description: Own the streaming data platform built on Kafka and Spark and used by analytics and ML teams for near-real-time workloads. Drive contract-first data delivery with upstream product teams, and mentor senior ICs on schema evolution and operational maturity.

title: Staff Data Engineer
description: Architect the data platform supporting multi-team business intelligence and product analytics use cases. Set warehouse standards across orgs, drive adoption of platform-wide patterns, and partner with leadership on vendor strategy and capacity planning.

title: Principal Data Engineer
description: Set org-wide data engineering standards across ingestion, warehousing, and transformation tooling. Own vendor strategy and the data platform roadmap, and partner with executives on multi-year investments in data infrastructure and team growth.
